title: The 11 Best Herbal Tinctures for Reducing and Treating Shortness of Breath

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin (Rosemary) is good for shortness of breath because it helps to relax and open up the airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Alkylamides are good for shortness of breath because they help relax the muscles in the airways, allowing more air to pass through and improving breathing.
Flavonoids, also known as Rosemary, are good for shortness of breath because they help to relax airway muscles and improve lung function, which makes breathing easier.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tincture is an herbal remedy that may help with shortness of breath. This plant is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the airways and make breathing easier. When the airways are inflamed, they can become narrower, leading to shortness of breath. Echinacea purpurea tincture may help calm the inflammation and open up the airways, allowing for better airflow. It also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the lungs and airways from damage caused by free radicals. Some people find that taking Echinacea purpurea tincture helps reduce congestion and coughing, making it easier to breathe. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Echinacea purpurea tincture for shortness of breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tincture to help with shortness of breath, you will need fresh or dried rosemary leaves, a glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like olive or grapeseed oil, and a clean glass bottle with a dropper lid to store the tincture. First, fill the glass jar with rosemary leaves, making sure they are completely covered with the carrier oil. Next, place the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract the herbs' medicinal properties. After the soaking time, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops 2-3 times a day, mixed with water, to help alleviate shortness of breath. Keep in mind that tinctures are not regulated by the FDA and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for shortness of breath

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Glycyrrhizin is not found, it is actually found in Glycyrrhiza glabra.
Polyphenols, found in rosemary, are good for shortness of breath because they help to open up airways and improve blood flow to the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Flavonoids, also known as rosemary, are good for shortness of breath because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help open up airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tincture is a natural remedy that can help with shortness of breath. The main active ingredient in this tincture is glycyrrhizin, a powerful anti-inflammatory that reduces swelling in the airways. When you have shortness of breath, your airways might be inflamed, making it harder to breathe. Glycyrrhizin in the tincture reduces this inflammation, helping your airways to open up and making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful for people with conditions like asthma or chronic bronchitis, where the airways are constantly inflamed. The tincture also has a soothing effect on the body, which can help to calm down the respiratory system and make breathing easier.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tincture that helps with shortness of breath, start by gathering fresh or dried rosemary leaves. If using fresh leaves, dry them in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them for a few days. Next, combine one part of dried rosemary leaves with two parts of a solvent, such as 80-proof vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar. The jar should be filled to the top to ensure the herb is completely covered by the solvent. Secure the lid tightly and store the jar in a cool, dark place, such as a cupboard, for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the solvent extract the herb's properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops, taken sublingually (under the tongue), as needed to help alleviate shortness of breath.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for shortness of breath

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Bilobalide in <Rosmarinus officinalis> is good for shortness of breath because it helps relax airway muscles and improves oxygen flow to the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Flavonoids are good for shortness of breath because they help relax airway muscles and increase blood flow to the lungs, making it easier to breathe.
Quercetin, also known as rosemary, is good for shortness of breath because it helps to relax airway muscles, allowing for easier breathing and increased oxygen flow to the body.
Isorhapontigenin, commonly known as rosemary, is good for shortness of breath because it can help relax airway muscles and improve breathing by acting as a bronchodilator.
Kaempferol, also known as rosemary, is good for shortness of breath because it helps to relax the airways and improve breathing by reducing inflammation and opening up the air passages.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is used to help people with shortness of breath. This is because it improves blood flow to the lungs. When we breathe, oxygen-rich blood is sent from our heart to our lungs, where it picks up oxygen. If blood flow is poor, less oxygen reaches the lungs, and we feel short of breath. Ginkgo biloba helps open up blood vessels, allowing more blood to flow to the lungs. This increases oxygen supply, which in turn eases shortness of breath. The herb also has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and congestion in the airways, further making breathing easier. People with conditions like chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) or heart failure often use Ginkgo biloba tincture to alleviate their breathing difficulties.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, herbal tinctures that can help with shortness of breath, you will need some fresh or dried rosemary leaves, a clean glass jar with a lid, a carrier oil like olive or coconut oil, and a solvent like vodka or glycerin. Start by preparing your rosemary leaves by rinsing them with water and gently patting them dry with a paper towel. Then, place the rosemary leaves in the glass jar, filling it about 2/3 full. Next, pour the carrier oil over the rosemary leaves, making sure that they are completely covered. Seal the jar with its lid and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the rosemary infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. If you prefer a more potent tincture, you can use a solvent like vodka or glycerin to extract the active compounds from the rosemary leaves. Simply mix the solvent with the infused oil in a ratio of 1 part solvent to 2 parts infused oil, and let it sit for a few days before straining and discarding the solids. Your rosemary herbal tincture is now ready to use, and you can take it as needed to help alleviate shortness of breath.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for shortness of breath

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins may help with shortness of breath due to their ability to relax and dilate airway passages, making it easier to breathe by reducing inflammation and opening up airways, which can improve lung function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory conditions.

Triterpenoids are good for shortness of breath because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help relax the muscles in the airways, allowing for easier breathing.
Astragaloside is not found but in Astragalus, is good for shortness of breath because it helps to improve lung function and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus tincture is a herbal remedy that may help with shortness of breath. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Astragalus also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the lungs from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, it has been shown to improve the function of the cardiovascular system, which is responsible for delivering oxygen to the body's tissues. By improving blood flow and oxygen delivery, Astragalus membranaceus tincture may help alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath. It can also boost the immune system, which can help the body fight off infections that may be contributing to breathing difficulties. Some studies suggest that Astragalus may also have a direct effect on the airways, causing them to relax and open up, making it easier to breathe.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis, or rosemary, herbal tincture that may help with shortness of breath, you'll need a few basic ingredients and some simple equipment. Start by gathering fresh or dried rosemary leaves, a glass jar with a lid, a dark glass bottle, and a carrier oil like vodka or glycerin. Begin by filling the jar with the rosemary leaves, then pour enough of the carrier oil over the leaves to cover them completely. The general ratio is one part herbs to two parts oil, but feel free to adjust this to suit your needs. Next, seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for about two weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the herbs infuse into the oil. After two weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, squeezing the cheesecloth to get as much of the liquid out of the herbs as possible. You can then use this rosemary tincture as needed, taking a few drops sublingually (under the tongue) to help alleviate shortness of breath.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal tinctures for shortness of breath

Shogaols are good for shortness of breath because they help relax the muscles in the airways, making it easier to breathe.
Curcumenol, also known as rosemary, is good for shortness of breath because its anti-inflammatory properties help to relax airway muscles and improve lung function, making it easier to breathe.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that can help with shortness of breath. The tincture form of ginger is easily absorbed into the body and can help to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to open up airways and improve circulation, allowing more oxygen to reach the lungs. This can be especially helpful for people with conditions like bronchitis or asthma, where inflammation and congestion can make it difficult to breathe. The tincture can be taken as needed, providing quick relief from shortness of breath. Additionally, ginger has been shown to have a positive effect on the digestive system, which can also impact breathing, as a healthy gut is essential for overall health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis (rosemary) tincture that helps with shortness of breath, start by gathering fresh or dried rosemary leaves. You will need a clean glass jar with a lid, a piece of cheesecloth or a coffee filter, and a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid. Fill the glass jar with the rosemary leaves, covering them with a neutral-tasting liquid such as vodka or glycerin. The general ratio is 1 part herb to 2 parts liquid. Close the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the liquid extract the herbs' properties. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into the dark glass bottle, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in the refrigerator to keep it fresh and use 10-20 drops under the tongue as needed, ideally before meals or before activities that may trigger shortness of breath. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have a pre-existing medical condition or are taking medications.

Polyphenols, also known as rosemary, may help with shortness of breath because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can improve lung function and reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe.

Ginsenoside rg1 (which is actually a compound found in Panax ginseng, not Rosmarinus officinalis which is Rosemary) is good for shortness of breath because it has anti-inflammatory properties and can improve cardiovascular function, which helps to increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues and reduce symptoms of shortness of breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tincture is a natural remedy that may help with shortness of breath. It contains ginsenosides, compounds that can help improve lung function and increase oxygen levels in the body. This can be especially helpful for people who have conditions like chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) or asthma. Panax ginseng tincture may also help reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, it can help boost energy levels and reduce fatigue, which can be a common symptom of shortness of breath. Some people also use Panax ginseng tincture to help manage stress and anxiety, which can contribute to breathing difficulties. It is often taken in small amounts, usually mixed with water or another liquid, before meals or before bedtime.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tincture that helps with shortness of breath, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, gather fresh or dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves from a trusted herbal supplier, making sure they are free from any contaminants. Next, fill a clean glass jar with about 1/4 cup of the Rosmarinus leaves. Then, add a solvent such as vodka or glycerin to the jar, covering the leaves completely. The general ratio is 1 part leaves to 2 parts solvent. Make sure the jar is airtight and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This process is called maceration and helps to extract the active compounds from the Rosmarinus leaves. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Your Rosmarinus tincture is now ready to use. It can be taken sublingually (under the tongue) or added to water, and the recommended dose is usually 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tincture is a natural remedy that can help with shortness of breath. The main reason is that it opens up airways in the lungs, making it easier to breathe. The tincture contains a chemical called eucalyptol, which is a natural decongestant that helps reduce swelling in the airways. When airways are congested, it's harder to breathe, but eucalyptol helps to clear out the mucus and debris that's blocking the airways. This makes it easier for oxygen to reach the lungs and for carbon dioxide to be removed, which can help to relieve shortness of breath. Eucalyptus globulus tincture is often used to help with respiratory problems, such as bronchitis, asthma, and chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD), and it's also used to relieve coughs and congestion. When taken in small amounts, it can be a safe and effective way to help manage shortness of breath and other respiratory issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis (rosemary) herbal tincture that may help with shortness of breath, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. First, dry a few sprigs of fresh rosemary leaves or use dried rosemary from a store. Next, combine the rosemary with a neutral solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, making sure the rosemary is completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part rosemary to 2 parts solvent, but feel free to adjust this to your liking. Now, store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the solvent extract the rosemary's active compounds. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your rosemary tincture is now ready to use, typically taken in a dose of 20-30 drops under the tongue or added to a warm drink, to help alleviate shortness of breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica tinctures have been used for centuries to help people with breathing problems. When we have shortness of breath, our airways can become narrow and swollen, making it harder to breathe. Ephedrine in Ephedra sinica tinctures helps to relax the muscles in the airways, making them wider and allowing more air to pass through. This can help to relieve congestion and make it easier to breathe. Additionally, ephedrine can also help to increase oxygen levels in the body, which can be beneficial for people with conditions like chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) or asthma. When used in moderation and under the guidance of a healthcare professional, Ephedra sinica tinctures may help to alleviate symptoms of shortness of breath and improve respiratory function.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, herbal tincture that helps with shortness of breath, start by gathering fresh rosemary leaves. Choose organic, fragrant leaves from the top of the plant. Next, combine 1 cup of rosemary leaves with 2 cups of 80% vodka or a high-proof spirit in a clean glass jar. Make sure the jar is big enough for the leaves to be fully submerged in the liquid. Seal the jar tightly and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. Discard the rosemary leaves and store the tincture in a cool, dark place. The rosemary tincture can be taken by mixing a few drops with water or taken sublingually (under the tongue) to help alleviate shortness of breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus tincture, also known as mullein, is a natural remedy that can help with shortness of breath. The tincture is made from the flowers and leaves of the mullein plant, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The main active ingredient in mullein is a compound called saponin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. When inhaled, the saponins in mullein tincture can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the airways, making it easier to breathe. This can be especially helpful for people with conditions such as bronchitis, asthma, or chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD). By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the airway muscles, mullein tincture can help to ease shortness of breath and improve overall lung function. Additionally, mullein's expectorant properties can help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, further aiding in relief from shortness of breath. The tincture can be taken internally as a cough syrup or added to a humidifier to inhale its benefits, providing quick and effective relief from shortness of breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tincture that helps with shortness of breath, start by gathering a handful of fresh or dried rosemary leaves. If using fresh leaves, tie them in a small bunch and hang them upside down in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks to dry completely. If using dried leaves, skip this step. Next, fill a clean glass jar with the dried or dried leaves, leaving about an inch of space at the top. Pour high-proof vodka or glycerin over the leaves, making sure they are completely covered. The general ratio is 1 part herbs to 2-3 parts liquid. Stir the mixture well and seal the jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process is called maceration, which allows the plant's active compounds to infuse into the liquid. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Your rosemary tincture is now ready to use. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture in water, 2-3 times a day, to help alleviate shortness of breath. However, please consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, is a herb that has been used for centuries to help with breathing problems. The tincture, which is a liquid extract, can be taken to help alleviate shortness of breath. Rosemary has natural decongestant properties that help to clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Rosmarinus officinalis herbal tinctures that help with shortness of breath, start by gathering fresh or dried rosemary leaves. Harvest the leaves in the early morning, when the oil content is highest. Clean the leaves thoroughly and dry them in a warm place, away from direct sunlight. Once the leaves are dry, use a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle to finely chop them. Combine the chopped rosemary with a neutral solvent like vodka or glycerin in a clean glass jar, with a ratio of 1 part herb to 2 parts solvent. Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to help the solvent extract the herb's active compounds. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. The resulting tincture can be taken as needed, mixed with water or taken directly, to help alleviate shortness of breath.

intro_desc: When you have shortness of breath, it means your body is not getting enough oxygen. This can be caused by various factors such as allergies, asthma, or even chronic conditions like COPD. Herbal tinctures can help alleviate this issue by providing natural relief. Two herbs that are commonly used for this purpose are Echinacea purpurea and Glycyrrhiza glabra.  Echinacea purpurea is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the airways and make it easier to breathe. It also has immune-boosting properties, which can help fight off infections that can trigger shortness of breath. On the other hand, Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help relax the airways and improve breathing.  Fixing shortness of breath is crucial because it can severely limit your daily activities. If you have trouble breathing, you may avoid exercising, socializing, or even going out in public. This can lead to feelings of isolation and depression. By addressing this issue, you can regain control over your life and enjoy activities that bring you joy. Improving your breathing can also increase your energy levels and overall well-being. You can improve your life by incorporating herbal tinctures into your daily routine, exercising regularly, and practicing stress-reducing techniques like meditation or deep breathing.
supplementary_best: A combination of Echinacea purpurea, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Ephedra sinica tinctures may help alleviate shortness of breath. Echinacea purpurea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the lungs and airways, making it easier to breathe. Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, has a soothing effect on the respiratory system and can help calm inflammation. Ephedra sinica, or ma huang, is a natural bronchodilator that can help open up airways, allowing more air to enter the lungs. The combination of these three herbs may provide a synergistic effect, addressing both the inflammation and airway constriction that can cause shortness of breath.  The specific ratio of the combination is 1 part Echinacea purpurea, 2 parts Glycyrrhiza glabra, and 3 parts Ephedra sinica. This ratio allows for a balanced effect of the anti-inflammatory properties of Echinacea purpurea, the soothing effect of Glycyrrhiza glabra, and the bronchodilatory effect of Ephedra sinica. The tinctures should be taken 2-3 times a day, diluted with water, to avoid any adverse effects. It is also recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you have any pre-existing medical conditions or are taking any medications.
supplementary_avoid: If you have shortness of breath, there are some herbal tinctures you should avoid. One of these is Yerba Santa (Eriodictyon glutinosum) tincture. Yerba Santa can cause bronchospasm, which is a tightening of the airways that can make breathing even more difficult. This can be a problem if you already have trouble breathing.  Another tincture to avoid is Lobelia (Lobelia inflata) extract. Lobelia can also cause bronchospasm and can be toxic in large amounts. It can cause symptoms such as rapid heartbeat, dizziness, and nausea. If you have shortness of breath, adding these symptoms can make it harder to breathe.  You should also be careful with Comfrey (Symphytum officinale) root tincture. Comfrey can cause lung damage and scarring, which can make breathing problems worse. If you have shortness of breath, using Comfrey can make it harder to breathe in the long run.  Another herbal tincture to avoid is Black Cohosh (Actaea racemosa) root tincture. While it is often used for women's health, Black Cohosh can cause respiratory problems and worsen shortness of breath. It can also interact with other medications and cause side effects.  Finally, you should avoid Blue Cohosh (Caulophyllum thalictroides) root tincture. Like Black Cohosh, Blue Cohosh can cause respiratory problems and worsen shortness of breath. It can also interact with other medications and cause side effects.
supplementary_other_remedies: For people experiencing shortness of breath, certain natural remedies can be helpful. One such remedy is inhaling steam from a bowl of hot water. When we breathe in hot steam, it helps to loosen and clear out mucus from our airways, making it easier to breathe. This is because the heat from the steam helps to thin out the mucus, allowing it to be coughed up more easily. Another remedy is drinking warm liquids, such as tea or broth, which can also help to thin out mucus and make breathing easier. This is because the warmth of the liquid helps to relax the muscles in our airways, allowing them to open up more easily.  In addition to these remedies, some people find that using a neti pot to rinse out their nasal passages can also help with shortness of breath. When we use a neti pot, we are essentially flushing out any irritants or mucus that may be blocking our airways. This can help to improve our breathing by allowing more air to pass through our nasal passages and into our lungs. Some people also find that using a humidifier in their home can be helpful, as it adds moisture to the air and can help to thin out mucus. This can be especially helpful for people who live in dry climates or have respiratory conditions that make it difficult to breathe.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who have shortness of breath may also experience other health issues. One common problem is fatigue. This is because the body is not getting enough oxygen, making it difficult to do daily tasks. Medicinal herbs like Ashwagandha can help reduce fatigue. It has anti-inflammatory properties that improve blood flow and increase oxygen supply to the body.  Another issue people with shortness of breath may face is anxiety. This is because the body is under stress, making it hard to breathe. Herbs like Lavender and Chamomile have a calming effect, which can help reduce anxiety. They also promote relaxation, making it easier to breathe.  People with shortness of breath may also experience insomnia. This is because they may have trouble falling asleep or staying asleep due to difficulty breathing. Herbs like Valerian root can help with sleep. It has a sedative effect, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.  In addition, people with shortness of breath may experience muscle weakness. This is because the body is not getting enough oxygen, making it difficult to move. Herbs like Ginkgo Biloba can help improve circulation and increase oxygen supply to the muscles. This can help reduce muscle weakness.  People with shortness of breath may also experience dizziness or lightheadedness. This is because the body is not getting enough oxygen, making it difficult to stay steady. Herbs like Peppermint can help improve circulation and reduce dizziness. It also has a cooling effect, which can help reduce inflammation in the body.  Finally, people with shortness of breath may experience a dry cough. This is because the body is producing mucus to help clear the airways. Herbs like Thyme and Echinacea can help reduce inflammation in the airways and promote mucus production. They also have antimicrobial properties, which can help fight off infections.
